evals.prompt: I want to debug this Tauri app's WebView. How do I inspect the DOM and check console logs with Chrome DevTools MCP?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide covering CDP port activation (env var), Chrome DevTools MCP connection (--browserUrl), list of available capabilities, and production security warnings
assertions: Includes CDP port activation method (WEBVIEW2_ADDITIONAL_BROWSER_ARGUMENTS env var or additionalBrowserArgs config)
States that the env var is process-level or shows it inline in the launch command
Includes Chrome DevTools MCP connection method (--browserUrl http://127.0.0.1:9222 or similar)

evals.prompt: I want to run a Lighthouse accessibility audit on a Tauri app. Can I do it directly on WebView2? If not, what's the alternative?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of Lighthouse limitations on WebView2 CDP (per category), note that accessibility is DOM-based so it works on direct connection, and alternative of opening localhost:1420 in Chrome with mock injection for full Lighthouse
assertions: States that full Lighthouse audit is limited on WebView2 CDP
Distinguishes that Accessibility is DOM-based and works on WebView2
Explains why the Performance category is limited
Suggests the browser-direct alternative (localhost dev server)
Mentions that invoke() mocks are needed

evals.prompt: I want to do UI automation testing on this Tauri app with Playwright MCP. How do I set it up and what should I watch out for?
evals.expected_output: CDP port activation -> Playwright MCP cdp://localhost:9222 connection method, target page selection in multi-window (main + overlay) environments, and limitations note that native APIs like invoke() cannot be tested
assertions: Includes Playwright MCP CDP connection method (cdp://localhost:9222 or similar)
Lists available Playwright MCP capabilities (at least 3)
Mentions target selection caveats in multi-window environments
Mentions that Tauri native APIs like invoke() cannot be tested
